key_insight: CatRetriever learns a shared latent space that lets a slab-level catalyst query retrieve plausible parent bulk crystals, closing the gap between surface generation and bulk material identification.
engineering_relevance: This enables generative catalyst discovery workflows to screen candidates not only for surface activity but also for bulk-dependent feasibility, including stability, symmetry, and synthesizability.
method: Contrastive learning aligns slab and bulk crystal representations in a common embedding space.
A slab query is used to retrieve candidate parent bulk structures from a reference database.
An extended pipeline combines retrieval with generative search expansion and adsorption-energy distribution analysis.
evidence_signal: The model reports strong retrieval performance, with R@1 above 91% and R@3 above 98% on both in-distribution and holdout evaluation sets.
watch_item: Retrieval quality depends on the completeness and bias of the bulk reference space, so missing or rare crystal families could limit parent-structure recovery.
paper_title: CatRetriever: Contrastive Representation Learning for Slab-to-Bulk Retrieval in Generative Catalyst Discovery
